meta.description: Personally Identifiable Information in Learning Objects — a dataset and tagset for PII detection and de-identification in educational text (student essays and forum posts).

summary.note: Majority of citations are peripheral — appearing in book chapters and broad reviews. Strongest engagements come from researchers working on PII detection, pseudonymization, and de-identification. Growing international visibility in the privacy/NLP space.

papers.title: Utility-Preserving De-Identification for Math Tutoring: Investigating Numeric Ambiguity in the MathEd-PII Benchmark Dataset
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — introduces a new domain-specific PII benchmark; PIILO likely cited as a related dataset or methodological touchpoint
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. Addresses PII detection in math tutoring transcripts where numeric expressions cause over-redaction — a domain-specific extension of the problem space PIILO operates in. Cornell authorship adds institutional weight.

papers.title: Evaluating LLM-based Personal Information Extraction and Countermeasures
papers.use_of_dataset: PIILO referenced as an existing study
papers.key_takeaway: LLMs outperform traditional methods at extracting PII; prompt injection can mitigate attacks. PIILO referenced as an existing study.

papers.title: Ethics and Transparency in Secure Web Model Generation
papers.use_of_dataset: Unclear — likely a background/reference citation; no direct experimental use evident
papers.key_takeaway: Weak connection to PIILO. Broad ethics-in-AI discussion; citation appears incidental.

papers.title: De-identifying Student PII in Discussion Forum Posts with LLMs
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct reference — PIILO's two studies (Holmes et al. 2023a and 2023b) cited as the foundational prior work that directly motivates this research
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. Zambrano et al. explicitly build on PIILO's de-identification findings from student essays and forum data, positioning their work as a direct extension into MOOC contexts with LLMs.

papers.title: The Conundrum of Trustworthy Research on Attacking PII Removal Techniques
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference — discusses limitations of existing PII datasets and evaluation setups
papers.key_takeaway: Existing PII removal evaluations are overestimated due to data leakage. Argues only truly private data can objectively evaluate vulnerabilities — directly relevant to PIILO's design rationale.

papers.title: Pseudonymization Categories across Domain Boundaries
papers.use_of_dataset: Reference/comparison — tagset analyzed alongside others, not used experimentally
papers.key_takeaway: No existing tagset fully covers cross-domain PII types at pseudonymization granularity. Advocates for a universal tagging system.

papers.title: 4L: An Architecture for AI-Augmented Learning
papers.use_of_dataset: Unclear — likely incidental citation; no direct experimental use evident from abstract
papers.key_takeaway: Very weak connection to PIILO. Focus is on data architecture for personalized learning; citation appears tangential.

papers.title: Risk Assessment and Mitigation With Generative AI Models
papers.key_takeaway: Weak connection to PIILO. Focus is on GANs/VAEs for cyber threat detection; citation appears peripheral.

papers.title: Safeguarding the Future: Advancements in Cybersecurity via Generative AI
papers.key_takeaway: Weak connection to PIILO. Broad generative AI in cybersecurity overview; citation appears incidental.

papers.title: Guest Editorial: The Datafication of Student Life in Higher Education
papers.key_takeaway: Weak connection to PIILO. Focuses on student data privacy ethics in learning analytics; citation appears peripheral.

papers.title: A Kubernetes-Based AI Framework for Scalable PII Detection and Redaction in Application Logs
papers.use_of_dataset: Unclear — uses AI4Privacy dataset for evaluation; PIILO likely a background/reference citation
papers.key_takeaway: Weak-to-moderate connection. Proposes Kubernetes-native PII redaction framework; PIILO not used directly but thematically relevant.

papers.title: RedacBench: Can AI Erase Your Secrets?
institutions: Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — introduces a new benchmark; PIILO possibly cited as a related dataset
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. Introduces policy-conditioned redaction benchmark across 514 texts and 187 security policies; thematically close to PIILO's goals.

papers.title: Opportunities and Risks in Utilizing AI in Education from a Data Science Perspective
papers.key_takeaway: Weak connection to PIILO. Broad AI-in-education risks overview; citation appears peripheral.

papers.title: Enhancing the De-identification of Personally Identifiable Information in Educational Data
papers.abstract: Examines GPT-4o-mini for detecting PII in educational settings using prompting and fine-tuning strategies. Fine-tuned model achieves recall of 0.9589 on CRAPII dataset with strong generalization across demographic backgrounds.
papers.use_of_dataset: Reference — PIILO cited as related work; primary evaluation uses CRAPII dataset
